title: "[SOLVED] iptables causing improper startup?"

### Post by sy88 on 2008-04-13
Hi, I've recently been trying to familiarize myself with iptables.  I've set up a few simple rules to test things out, but my computer runs into errors when I attempt to get the firewall to load on startup.  Here is my iptables.rules that I want to load on startup:```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.8 on Sat Apr 12 22:58:08 2008
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [72032:12125615]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [121970:47294080]
-A INPUT -m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --sport 1024:65535 --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -i eth0 -p tcp -m tcp --sport 1024:65535 -m multiport --dports 80,443 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -j DROP
COMMIT
# Completed on Sat Apr 12 22:58:08 2008

```And here's my /etc/network/interfaces:```
auto lo
iface lo inet loopback

auto eth0
iface eth0 inet static
address 192.168.1.150
netmask 255.255.255.0
gateway 192.168.1.1
#pre-up iptables-restore < /etc/network/iptables.rules

```  Uncommenting "pre-up iptables-restore < /etc/network/iptables.rules" causes Ubuntu to load up but show a blank orange screen after logging in and then a small white empty box (which I presume is an incomplete render of a terminal).  Could someone show me what I'm doing wrong or tell me how to correctly configure iptables to load on startup?  Thanks.
